# Transcrição: 59. Trait 58 - Great Salespeople know the difference between selling, negotiating and closing.

- **Fonte:** 59. Trait 58 - Great Salespeople know the difference between selling, negotiating and closing.
- **Duração:** 01:27
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:27]

Great salespeople know the difference between selling and negotiating and closing. Look, these are completely different arts. You know, average and good salespeople don't know the difference. They blend the two together. They're like, oh yes, selling, closing, negotiating. These are all the same thing. It's one art. No, it's not. It's completely different. Just because you can use a hammer doesn't mean you can use a drill. You know, just because you can present your product doesn't mean you know how to close a transaction. They're completely different and should be learned differently. Great salespeople want to study the two sciences and maybe the three selling, negotiating and closing. They're different things. Negotiating is not closing. It's making sense of something. Selling is, you know, showing the product, a service, the proposition, why it's valuable. Negotiating is coming to an agreement on it, not discounting it. Negotiating doesn't mean I lower it. It means we agree on something. And closing is different than those two. Closing is signed right there. Let's do this. Make a decision, roll, lean on the pin. Let's go and then handling all the emotional stuff and the uncertainty that comes up, you know, as a result of that. Look, great salespeople know the difference. If you don't know the difference, clearly know the difference. Understand this. That's a good thing. If you don't know the difference, you can't be great. Selling, negotiating, closing, learn them all and be great at all three of them because there are three completely different skill sets.
